content.correction: Leo has been executing tasks directly instead of delegating to Specialists. This violates the core architecture.
content.how_to_apply: Before executing any task, ask: is this orchestration/judgment, or execution? If execution → delegate to a Specialist via the Agent tool. Leo only executes directly for meta-work: routing, propagation, memory management, synthesis for user.
content.rule: Leo (Opus) orchestrates and delegates to Specialists (Sonnet) for execution. In 90%+ of cases, Leo should NOT execute tasks directly — he routes, judges, and synthesizes. Execution belongs to Specialists.
content.why: When Leo executes directly, it wastes Opus tokens on work Sonnet can handle and undermines the delegation architecture. The old Managers layer was removed — the new model is Leo ↔ Specialists (ephemeral, Sonnet-powered).
